title: High Level Board that connects items from various low level boards based on due date
excerpt: I would like to create a high level board that pulls ITEMS from a number of low level boards into groups like “Past Due”, “This Week”, “Next Week”, “Nex Month”, etc.  so that the team can see near future tasks that other…

posts.author: Pi300 (Michelle)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would like to create a high level board that pulls ITEMS from a number of low level boards into groups like “Past Due”, “This Week”, “Next Week”, “Nex Month”, etc. so that the team can see near future tasks that others are working on. Similar to what the “My Work” does for personal tasks.

Example of how the low level boards would be organized:

image2977×847 124 KB

Example of what I’d like the high level board to be, just selected columns are mirrored:

image1818×1271 148 KB

Is this possible?

posts.author: garfield-labs (Snir Alon)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Michelle - yes, this is possible with Same Item Multiple Boards app - there’s an automation that goes “when date has arrived, add the same item to board in group”. You can set the date with “1 day before date arrived/1 week after date arrives” etc

posts.author: Jacques1 (Skylar Reynolds)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Pi300 — great question! What you’re describing is possible in monday.com with a few smart setups. You can create a high-level “Summary” board that pulls items from multiple low-level boards using the Connect Boards + Mirror Columns combination. While monday doesn’t automatically group items by date range (like “Past Due” or “Next Week”) out of the box, you can achieve this with automations and filters:

  1. Connect Boards: On your high-level board, use the Connect Boards column to link all your low-level boards.

  2. Mirror Key Columns: Mirror the Item Name, Due Date, Owner, and Status columns.

  3. Automations: Use date-based automations or dashboard widgets to automatically group or filter items (e.g., “If Due Date is within the next 7 days → Move item to ‘This Week’ group”).

  4. Dashboards as an Alternative: You can also use a Dashboard with a Table widget or Timeline widget pulling data across boards. This gives you a My Work–style overview for all users based on due dates.

While it won’t be 100% automatic like My Work, this approach provides a similar cross-board visibility for your team.

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Michelle,

You can try the Mirror Item Multiple Boards app.

181920×960 188 KB

You can add an automation for pulling items from multiple low level boards based on the ‘date’ as a trigger.

What You Can Do with Mirror Item -

Add the SAME ITEM ON MULTIPLE BOARDS with live sync and full context

Sync UPDATES + REPLIES in real time for items and subitems

Set up automations like “When status changes to something, mirror item to target board and group”

Create a Master Board with items/subitems from multiple boards

Add a subitem from one board as a main item on another board

Keep your boards clean and connected — no Mirror Columns or Connect Boards needed

If you’d like a FREE DEMO, feel free to reach out to us at support@fortimus.co.

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you want a high-level view that automatically pulls tasks from multiple boards and groups them into “Past Due”, “This Week”, or “Next Week”, try Time in Status for monday.com .
It basically acts like an advanced “My Work” for teams - you can pull in items from all boards, filter by due date or status, and even see how long tasks have been in each phase. Makes weekly planning a lot smoother.
